# SettleWire

> Prediction markets news, contextualized. We cluster coverage of each event
> from 90+ outlets, credit the first reporter, and add original analysis of
> why it matters for traders, operators, and regulation watchers. All article
> text is our own original writing; every story links to its primary source.

## Sections
- [Front page](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/index.html)
- [Story archive](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/all.html): every story, searchable
- [The Docket](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/events.html): upcoming court dates, earnings, deadlines
- [RSS feed](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/rss.xml)

## Latest stories
- [Polymarket Files for US Margin Trading License on Event Contracts](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/polymarket-files-for-us-margin-trading-license-on-event-contracts.html): Polymarket has submitted regulatory applications to offer margin trading on prediction-market contracts to US users, marking its most direct push yet toward legal domestic operations.
- [Wall Street Banks Ban Staff from Trading Prediction Markets](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/wall-street-banks-ban-staff-from-trading-prediction-markets.html): Major Wall Street banks have issued internal rules prohibiting employees from trading on prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket, citing conflicts of interest and compliance concerns, according to Reuters.
- [Davis Wright Tremaine: Insider Trading Risk Now Hits Prediction Markets](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/davis-wright-tremaine-insider-trading-risk-now-hits-prediction-markets.html): Law firm Davis Wright Tremaine published a compliance alert warning that insider trading rules now materially apply to prediction markets, urging platforms and participants to update their programs immediately.
- [Goldman, firms grapple with insider trading rules as prediction markets grow](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/goldman-firms-grapple-with-insider-trading-rules-as-prediction-markets-grow.html): Goldman Sachs and other major companies are actively developing internal policies to address insider trading risks posed by employee participation in prediction markets, according to CNBC.
- [Kalshi market prices 50% chance Fed raises rates in 2026](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/kalshi-market-prices-50-chance-fed-raises-rates-in-2026.html): Traders on Kalshi's prediction market are pricing approximately 50-50 odds of a Federal Reserve rate hike occurring sometime in 2026, reflecting deep uncertainty as Fed policymakers remain publicly divided on the path forward.
- [Kalshi traders bet gas prices stay elevated amid U.S.-Iran tensions](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/kalshi-traders-bet-gas-prices-stay-elevated-amid-u-s-iran-tensions.html): Kalshi event-contract traders are pricing in persistently higher U.S. gasoline prices through mid-2025, driven by renewed geopolitical risk tied to escalating U.S.-Iran tensions, according to CNBC.
- [Arizona Gov. Hobbs Signs Executive Order Targeting Prediction Market Insider Trading](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/arizona-gov-hobbs-signs-executive-order-targeting-prediction-market-insider-trad.html): Arizona Governor Katie Hobbs issued an executive order targeting insider trading on prediction markets, making Arizona the first state to directly regulate information abuse on event-contract platforms.
- [Kalshi appeals federal ruling backing New York gambling regulators](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/kalshi-appeals-federal-ruling-backing-new-york-gambling-regulators.html): Kalshi has filed an appeal after a federal judge ruled in favor of New York gambling regulators who sought to restrict the prediction-market platform's sports event contracts in the state.
- [Kalshi Weather Delay Contracts Draw Regulatory Scrutiny](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/kalshi-weather-delay-contracts-draw-regulatory-scrutiny.html): Kalshi's new prediction markets allowing bettors to wager on weather-related sports delays have drawn regulatory questions about whether such contracts fall within permissible CFTC-sanctioned event contract boundaries.
- [CAP urges ban on government insiders trading prediction markets](https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/settlewire/story/cap-urges-ban-on-government-insiders-trading-prediction-markets.html): The Center for American Progress published a policy brief calling for prohibitions on government officials trading prediction markets, citing national-security risks from insider-information advantages.
